chunk_summary: This section discusses micronutrient supplementation and infection prevention in managing moderate acute malnutrition (MAM), emphasizing integration with public health programs like Anaemia Mukt Bharat, National Deworming Day, and Vitamin A prophylaxis schedules.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of public health programs in addressing micronutrient deficiencies in MAM.
Learn the prophylactic iron-folic acid (IFA) supplementation schedule for different age groups under Anaemia Mukt Bharat.
Recognize the importance of deworming and Vitamin A supplementation in infection prevention for children with MAM.
Identify key infection-prevention measures to reinforce during MAM outpatient visits.
key_facts: Anaemia Mukt Bharat provides IFA syrup (20 mg iron, 100 mcg folic acid) twice weekly for ages 6–59 months.
Albendazole is administered as a single 200 mg dose for ages 1–2 years and 400 mg for ages 2–19 years under National Deworming Day.
Vitamin A prophylaxis includes 100,000 IU at 9 months and 200,000 IU every 6 months up to 5 years.
Zinc supplementation for acute diarrhoea is 20 mg daily for 10–14 days (10 mg daily if under 6 months).
MAM visits should verify immunization status and promote handwashing and safe water practices.
reference_quote: Anaemia Mukt Bharat: prophylactic IFA supplementation schedule — https://nhm.gov.in/index1.php?lang=1&level=3&lid=797&sublink...
